feed.title: 4chan
feed.subtitle: Newest threads from selected worksafe 4chan boards (news, g, o, tv, v, mu, vip) via the read-only JSON API, plus the official 4chan blog. Board posts are user-generated and unmoderated; each title is prefixed with its board.

entry.title: /o/ Auto: Repair in parking lot?
entry.content: Timing belt is loose, pulley or other belt gave way on freeway. Towed to parking lot in bumfuck nowhere. 520km from home, getting a bus home tonight. The car cost 3300 AUD 6 years and is interference design, I think it's likely no internal damaged was suffered since shut off was immediate, but not 100% Would you: A) Repair with a timing belt kit in parking lot, about 300-400 AUD + 6 hour bus ride back to car after getting it after who knows how long B) Tow it home for $1479 AUD, then try to repa

entry.title: /g/ Technology: /tpg/ - ThinkPad General
entry.content: >>108884792 Don't buy anything other than IBM/Lenovo ThinkPad T, X, and W/P Series if you want the Real Business Experience™ Other business laptops are welcome in /tpg/ (Dell Latitude/Precision, HP EliteBook/ZBook) Why ThinkPad? >Used machines are plentiful and cheap >Utilitarian design: e.g. mix of old and new ports, stable keyboard layout >Easy to repair, upgrade (some models) & maintain thanks to readily available service manuals, spare parts easy to obtain >Usable in cramped quarters thanks

entry.title: /g/ Technology: The State of Linux Gaming
entry.content: Hello /g/. After a year of being in the Linux rabbithole I have switched back to Windows 11 on my main PC. Linux was great but unfortunately I ran into frequent issues with crashing and instability while playing vidya. On a scale of 1-10 how much of a faggot am I for this? >inb4 blog post Do any anons use linux full time for gaming and if so what OS and Desktop Environment? Do you think Linux is ready for gamers to make the switch? Should I consider trying out Linux again? >inb4 stop playing vid

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Chipset-free motherboards
entry.content: Is there a reason we aren't seeing the market develop lower-end motherboards with reduced I/O and no chipsets to keep the costs down? Most "Gamers" don't need 6 SATA ports, 16 USB ports, Wifi, 2x PCI-E X16 slots, etc. And all modern x86 CPUs are actually SoCs that can run without chipsets, technically. I started building a layout for a MATX board without a chipset and no video ports, just a couple of USB-A and C ports and a 2.5Gbe adapter, standard PCI-E X16 5.0, M.2 @ 5.0 speeds as well. The bi

entry.title: /g/ Technology: A tech product is not "private" if they comply with a government subpoena, sear…
entry.content: A tech product is not "private" if they comply with a government subpoena, search warrant, or court order. They could literally make it impossible to recover data by default. So even if a government requests it, they could just say they don't have it.

entry.title: /g/ Technology: I'm really impressed by pure-object languages like Self and Smalltalk.
entry.content: I don't have enough experience to judge the difference between functional and OO. But OO seems much more attractive to me for some reason. Originally I just preferred procedural programming and thought OO was just an overhead but after seeing a nice implementation of it with Smalltalk and Self it changed my mind on OO enough to really admire it. The idea that programs are "objects" and communicate through messages in a live space environment (live image) just seems like something from the future

entry.title: /g/ Technology: /pcbg/ - PC Building General
entry.content: >UPGRADE & BUILD ADVICE Post build list or current specs: https://pcpartpicker.com/ Provide specific use cases State BUDGET and COUNTRY or you will NOT be helped >CASE mATX: AP201, Lian Li A3, O11 Air Mini, XT M3, CH260 ATX: XT PRO (ULTRA), AIR 903 Base/MAX, Lancool 217, Flux Pro, Meshify 3, 4000D FRAME, X50 Dual Chamber: Y60/70, O11 Vision, Antec C8 >CPU Gaming: 250K, 14600K, 7/9600X, 7/9800X3D -Budget: 225F, 7500F, 12600K Workstation: 270K, 9950X3D *Avoid Asrock motherboards on AM5 *On Raptor

entry.title: /news/ News: Iran has signaled trump will surrender to them and ned his war against the global economy
entry.content: Unlike trump's previous announcements to war profiteer, the Iranians have confirmed the deal. https://apnews.com/article/iran-us- war-ceasefire-deal-e0a9e4e1152ea8da 10ea066ad174a23a ISLAMABAD (AP) — The United States and Iran have reached an agreement to end the war and open the Strait of Hormuz, offering relief to the global economy more than three months since fighting began. Details of the deal were not immediately available and Iran signaled implementation would not start until the signing,

entry.title: /o/ Auto: Wanting to do more things on my own car?
entry.content: I wanted to try and experiment on fixing some things in my car. I wouldn't say I am not the most car savvy though. For example, I think the air conditioning in my car needs a recharge or whatever. Is that something I could feasibly do on my own or not? Not even sure how I'd investigate what I can and shouldn't do. For example, I'd probably never replace my own engine and I think it might burn oil. I have been checking the oil and putting oil in as it burns though. So stuff like that I can sort o

entry.title: /g/ Technology: OLED is a shitty dead end tech, when Micro-LED comes along, OLED will be irrelev…
entry.content: OLED is a shitty dead end tech, when Micro-LED comes along, OLED will be irrelevant. OLED fanboys swear it's the best thing, but it has too many downsides for deep blacks which Mini-LED comes very close to achieving without a million sacrifices in durability. You're paying a premium to be a tech tester.

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Google is scanning all your correspondance with AI
entry.content: Just thought I'd let you guys know that Google is using AI to scan all your emails. I know email isn't private and this isn't news, but you should know that it's happening. Go into your Gmail settings > All Settings > Smart Features and disable that shit. You also should disable "Google Workspace smart features".
